methodologies.name: OPEX Fitness
methodologies.philosophy: Individualized program design rooted in assessment-driven prescription. Train the individual, not the methodology. Lifestyle, sleep, nutrition, and stress are programmed alongside training.
methodologies.periodization: Annual macrocycle broken into Gain, Pain, Sustain phases. Mesocycles 4-12 weeks. Microcycles built around the individual's recovery capacity and life demands.

weekly_template.structure: 5 days on / 2 off OR 3 on / 1 off variants based on athlete profile
weekly_template.session_layout: Aerobic warm-up -> Skill/Strength -> Conditioning -> Aerobic cool-down or Z2 piece

methodologies.energy_system_approach: MAP 1-10 framework. MAP 1 = aerobic recovery (Z1-Z2). MAP 3-5 = aerobic power/threshold. MAP 6-8 = anaerobic lactic. MAP 9-10 = alactic power. Programmed by HR zones, RPE, and pacing rather than blind intensity.
methodologies.strength_approach: Percentage-based with bias toward absolute strength early in cycles, transitioning to strength-endurance and contextual strength (loaded carries, odd-object) as competition approaches. Tempo and pause work emphasized.
methodologies.accessory_work: Body Building (BB) blocks for structural balance. Posterior chain, single-leg, midline, and pulling volume targeted by individual assessment. 2-4 accessory pieces per week.
signature_elements: The Assessment - movement, body composition, energy system testing
Body Building blocks separated from metabolic work

athlete_tiers.intermediate: 5 days/week, structured strength + energy system blocks
athlete_tiers.competitor: 6 days/week with multiple sessions, individualized weakness focus, year-round periodization

methodologies.recovery_lifestyle: Sleep, nutrition, stress management programmed explicitly. RPE and HRV-informed deloads. Strong emphasis on year-round training age accumulation rather than peaking.

methodologies.name: HWPO (Hard Work Pays Off)
methodologies.philosophy: Brutal consistency. Identify weaknesses, attack them daily. The work that wins is the work nobody wants to do. High volume, high intensity, with structured deload to prevent burnout.
methodologies.periodization: 12-week cycles with a 4-week deload/recovery block built in. Linear progression on primary lifts. Mesocycles align to Open/Quarterfinals/Semifinals/Games calendar for competitors.

weekly_template.session_layout: Tabata buy-in -> Strength -> WOD -> Tabata buy-out -> Optional accessory

methodologies.energy_system_approach: Heavy emphasis on aerobic capacity and threshold. Long pieces (20-40 min) programmed weekly. Anaerobic lactic work concentrated mid-week. Tabata format used as aerobic 'sandwich' around main work.
methodologies.strength_approach: Percentage-based linear progression. Squat/Deadlift/Press/Snatch/Clean & Jerk cycled across the year. Heavy singles, doubles, triples in primary lifts. Weakness-specific accessory volume.
methodologies.accessory_work: Targeted weakness work prescribed by track. Pulling volume, midline, single-arm/single-leg, gymnastics-specific strength.
signature_elements: Track ecosystem: PRO, Flagship, STRONG, RUN

athlete_tiers.flagship: 5-6 days/week competitive CrossFit programming for intermediate/advanced
athlete_tiers.pro: Highest volume, multi-session days, Games-level athletes
athlete_tiers.strong: Strength-bias track for those wanting powerlifting/Olympic emphasis with conditioning
athlete_tiers.run: Endurance-focused track for runners or hybrid athletes

methodologies.recovery_lifestyle: Mandatory deload weeks. Sleep emphasized as #1 recovery tool. Mat Fraser openly programs recovery as 'work' equal to training.

methodologies.name: Training Think Tank
methodologies.philosophy: Develop the complete CrossFit athlete through balanced programming, deep movement understanding, and culture-driven gym training. Every athlete is built from FIVES: Strength, Stamina, Power, Skill, Mobility.
methodologies.periodization: A/B week split alternating focus blocks. Mesocycles built around the FIVES model. Macrocycles peak for Open/Quarterfinals/Semifinals/Games.

weekly_template.session_layout: Warm-up -> Skill/Movement Prep -> Strength -> Conditioning -> Accessory or Cooldown

methodologies.energy_system_approach: Balanced across all three. Aerobic threshold day is non-negotiable. Anaerobic intervals programmed mid-week. Alactic work paired with skill sessions.
methodologies.strength_approach: Olympic lift technical proficiency prioritized. Pause work, tempo work, and complex variations. Squat/press/pull volume periodized in 4-week waves.
methodologies.accessory_work: Movement-specific. Deep emphasis on positional integrity, hip/ankle/shoulder mobility, midline. Often programmed as 'Movement Snacks' (5-10 min focused work).
signature_elements: FIVES model (Strength, Stamina, Power, Skill, Mobility)

athlete_tiers.tier_1_foundations: Movement quality and base fitness; 3-4 days/week

methodologies.recovery_lifestyle: Sunday off mandatory. Movement quality over output - if positions break, scale. Mobility programmed in micro-doses daily.

methodologies.name: Mayhem Athlete
methodologies.philosophy: Train hard, train consistently, train with community. Mayhem culture is built on team-style training, faith, family, and fitness. Programming is varied, high-volume, and competition-tested.
methodologies.periodization: 5 named annual mesocycles: Freedom, Hulk Out, Into the Storm, Nerves of Steel, Nimble & Quick. Each cycle has distinct focus and intensity profile. Cycles align with Open, Quarterfinals, Semifinals, Games.

weekly_template.session_layout: Strength -> WOD -> Optional second session (extra credit)

methodologies.energy_system_approach: Heavy mixed modal volume. Aerobic capacity built through long team-style WODs. Anaerobic capacity through repeated sprint format. Alactic less emphasized - built from Olympic lifting load.
methodologies.strength_approach: Olympic lifting prioritized year-round. Squat strength built in dedicated cycles. Percentage-based with heavy Wednesday and heavy weekend tradition. Strict gymnastics strength integrated.
methodologies.accessory_work: Posterior chain, midline, and pulling volume. Often programmed as 'extra credit' optional pieces. Bodybuilding accessories occasionally programmed.
signature_elements: 10+ track ecosystem (Compete, Performance, Masters, Teens, Engine, Bodybuilding, etc.)
Faith and family integrated into culture

athlete_tiers.performance: Affiliate-level athletes, 4-5 days/week, Rx and scaled options
athlete_tiers.elite: Semifinals/Games-level, multi-session days, individualized weakness work
athlete_tiers.engine: Aerobic capacity track for hybrid/runner athletes

methodologies.recovery_lifestyle: One day off weekly. Sleep, nutrition, faith/family time emphasized as recovery pillars. Deload weeks built into mesocycle transitions.

methodologies.name: Misfit Athletics
methodologies.philosophy: Competitor-focused programming. Build relentless engines, brutal capacity, and competition-ready athletes. Train the work nobody else will do.
methodologies.periodization: Four 7-week phases per year + Comp Blocks aligned to Open/Quarterfinals/Semifinals. Phases build progressively toward competition peak. Comp Blocks shift to testing and competition-specific stimulus.

weekly_template.session_layout: Strength -> Bitch Work (aerobic) -> Conditioning -> Weakness Template piece

methodologies.energy_system_approach: Aerobic dominant. 'Bitch Work' is the signature aerobic capacity element - long, sustained, sub-threshold work that builds the engine. Anaerobic lactic concentrated 1-2 days/week. Alactic woven into strength work.
methodologies.strength_approach: Percentage-based with heavy bias toward absolute strength and contextual strength (under fatigue). Olympic lifting volume high. Squat and pull cycles run concurrent with conditioning blocks.
methodologies.accessory_work: Weakness Templates layer - athlete identifies gaps from assessment, picks weakness templates that overlay onto base programming. Common weaknesses: HSPU, MU, double-unders, breathing under load.

athlete_tiers.mft_full: Misfit Full programming, 6 days/week, full competitor volume
athlete_tiers.mft_competitor: Quarterfinals/Semifinals-level, multi-session days, weakness templates required

methodologies.recovery_lifestyle: Deload weeks at end of each 7-week phase. Sleep prioritized. Active recovery on rest day encouraged.

methodologies.name: CompTrain
methodologies.philosophy: Concurrent training - simultaneously develop strength, power, and engine. Mindset is the foundation of fitness. Train the person, not just the body.
methodologies.periodization: Concurrent model - all energy systems and strength qualities trained year-round with shifting emphasis. Open/Quarterfinals/Semifinals/Games peaks layered on top of base.

weekly_template.session_layout: Mindset Minute -> Warm-up -> Strength -> WOD -> Cooldown

methodologies.energy_system_approach: Concurrent - all three trained weekly. Aerobic threshold programmed 1-2x/week. Anaerobic lactic 1x/week. Alactic woven into strength sessions.
methodologies.strength_approach: Percentage-based. Squat, deadlift, press, Olympic lifts cycled. Tempo and pause work used for technical refinement. Heavy days prescribed weekly.
methodologies.accessory_work: Movement quality and 5-Tool development. Gymnastics strength (strict pulls, strict presses, midline) integrated.
signature_elements: Mindset Minute - daily mental performance prompt
5-Tool Athlete model (Strength, Stamina, Speed, Skill, Mindset)
Deep Health 5 pillars (Physical, Mental, Emotional, Existential, Relational)

athlete_tiers.comptrain_class: Affiliate-level, 5 days/week, scalable for all levels

methodologies.recovery_lifestyle: Mindset and Deep Health framework treats recovery as integral. Sleep, nutrition, relationships programmed as performance variables.

methodologies.name: PRVN Fitness
methodologies.philosophy: Proven programming. Built around what worked for the most decorated CrossFit athlete in history. Volume tolerance and technical proficiency are the two pillars.
methodologies.periodization: One-week-behind Compete model - PRVN Compete runs the actual programming Tia/team is doing one week behind real-time. Mesocycles align to competition calendar.

weekly_template.session_layout: Warm-up -> Strength -> WOD -> Accessory / Pump -> Optional engine piece

methodologies.energy_system_approach: Aerobic capacity prioritized - threshold and Z2 work programmed weekly. Anaerobic lactic via repeat sprint format. Alactic via Olympic lifting and short sprints.
methodologies.strength_approach: Percentage-based, Olympic lifting heavy. Squat and pull volume high. Tempo and complex work for technical proficiency.
methodologies.accessory_work: PRVN Pump track is dedicated bodybuilding accessory. Within Compete, accessories target Tia-style weakness work - midline, posterior chain, single-arm/single-leg.
signature_elements: Modular ecosystem: Compete, Burn, Pump, Engine, HYROX

athlete_tiers.prvn_compete: Competitive CrossFit, 6 days/week, three daily levels

methodologies.recovery_lifestyle: One day off per week. Sleep, nutrition, mobility emphasized. Tia's openly programmed recovery practices (sauna, contrast, mobility) referenced.

methodologies.name: Linchpin
methodologies.philosophy: Brutally elegant. Sustainable fitness for the long haul. Programming for the everyday athlete who wants to train hard, stay healthy, and still be doing this in 30 years.
methodologies.periodization: Non-periodized by design. No peaks, no testing weeks, no Open/Games focus. Daily variety, sustainable intensity, longevity bias.

weekly_template.session_layout: Warm-up -> Single WOD or Strength + WOD -> Cooldown

methodologies.energy_system_approach: Balanced. Time domains rotate naturally - some sub-5min days, some 20-40min days. No formal energy system periodization.
methodologies.strength_approach: Mixed - Olympic, powerlifting, gymnastics strength all rotate. No formal percentage waves. Heavy days when programmed but no peaking blocks.
methodologies.accessory_work: Minimal formal accessory. Movement quality through varied programming rather than dedicated accessory blocks.

athlete_tiers.linchpin_main: Single track for general fitness adults, scalable

methodologies.recovery_lifestyle: Weekends off. Sustainable pace prioritized over peak performance. Designed for athletes balancing training with full life.
